name: OPEC+
summary: Agreed modest 206K bpd increase for April but it's largely moot — most OPEC Gulf barrels are stranded if Hormuz stays shut. Only Saudi has real spare capacity. Saudi pre-boosted 640K bpd. Iraq declared force majeure.

signals.content: OPEC+ agreed to 206K bpd increase for April (debated range 137K-548K bpd). Modest because spare capacity limited and export routes threatened.
Actual OPEC output rose 640K bpd ahead of conflict (biggest increase since June), with Saudi accounting for ~50%. But export routes now blocked.

edges.context: OPEC+ controls ~40% of global oil production. Production decisions directly affect price. But with Hormuz closed, production increases can't reach market.
Saudi is de facto leader and only member with significant spare capacity (3-5M bpd).
Most OPEC Gulf production depends on Hormuz for export. Closure strands the barrels — production increases become moot.
